title: Dactylorhiza baltica
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza baltica is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Dactylorhiza, which is a group of terrestrial orchids commonly known as marsh orchids. The genus Dactylorhiza is further divided into several sections, with D. baltica being part of the Coeloglossum section. This species is native to Europe and is often found in wet meadows and along riverbanks. D. baltica is a rare and endangered orchid due to habitat destruction and other environmental factors.

intro: Dactylorhiza baltica, also known as the Baltic orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid belonging to the Dactylorhiza genus. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. The bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza baltica include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Dactylorhiza baltica is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in the form of dried root or fresh plant material, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
